description: Fix syntax error in src/map.js that prevents the game from launching. There is an extra closing brace '}' on line 296 (after the closing brace of the placePathCell function). This causes a module parse error that prevents the entire module graph from loading, so window.startNewGame is never assigned.
steps: Open src/map.js and locate the placePathCell function ending around lines 293-296. There are two consecutive closing braces '} }' where the second one is extraneous.
Remove the extra '}' on line 296 (the one that comes after the closing brace of placePathCell on line 295).
Run verification: for f in src/*.js game.js; do node --input-type=module --check < "$f" 2>&1; done - ALL files must pass with no errors.
acceptance_criteria: Running the same check on all other .js files also produces no errors
The game's module graph can be fully loaded without parse errors
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/Sandbox-td && for f in src/*.js game.js; do echo "=== $f ==="; node --input-type=module --check < "$f" 2>&1 || exit 1; done
findings: Removed the extra closing brace on line 296 of src/map.js. All JS files pass module syntax check successfully.
